  1. Implementation
  2. UI Screens

UI Screens

  • Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • View Source

Screen Overview

Four tab screens compose the mobile experience:

Show code 
graph LR
    G[Generate] -->|success| R[Result]
    G -->|no API key| S[Settings]
    R -->|back| G
    GL[Gallery] --> R

Home Screen (Generate)

The primary screen with prompt input, overlay text, resolution picker, and generate button.

Layout Structure

SafeAreaView
└── ScrollView (keyboardShouldPersistTaps)
    ├── Header (Sparkles icon + "NanoMeme")
    ├── Subtitle
    ├── Prompt Section
    │   ├── TextInput (multiline, 4 lines)
    │   └── "Load Yoda Preset" button
    ├── Overlay Text Section
    │   └── TextInput (single line)
    ├── Resolution Picker
    │   └── Row of 4 buttons (0.5K | 1K | 2K | 4K)
    ├── Error Card (conditional)
    └── Generate Button (with ActivityIndicator)

Generation Flow

const handleGenerate = useCallback(async () => {
  const apiKey = await getApiKey();
  if (!apiKey) {
    failGeneration("No API key configured...");
    router.navigate("/settings");
    return;
  }

  startGeneration();
  try {
    const result = await generateAndSaveMeme(
      apiKey,
      currentPrompt || DEFAULT_YODA_PROMPT,
      overlayText || DEFAULT_OVERLAY_TEXT,
    );
    completeGeneration(result.imageUri);
    router.navigate("/result");
  } catch (error) {
    failGeneration(
      error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Generation failed",
    );
  }
}, [currentPrompt, overlayText, ...]);

Key behaviors:

  1. No API key → Redirects to Settings tab
  2. Empty prompt → Falls back to Yoda preset prompt
  3. Success → Navigates to Result screen
  4. Error → Shows error card with red border

KaosMaps Styling

All input fields use the dark theme with cyan-tinted borders:

textArea: {
  backgroundColor: colors.surface.card,  // #1a1a24
  borderRadius: 12,
  padding: 14,
  color: colors.text.primary,            // #e2e8f0
  borderWidth: 1,
  borderColor: "rgba(6,182,212,0.15)",   // Cyan subtle border
},

Result Screen

Displays the generated meme with share/save/favorite actions.

KaosMaps Glow Effect

The image container features the signature KaosMaps glow:

glowOuter: {
  position: "absolute",
  top: -2, left: -2, right: -2, bottom: -2,
  borderRadius: 18,
  shadowColor: colors.brand.cyan,
  shadowOffset: { width: 0, height: 0 },
  shadowOpacity: 0.15,
  shadowRadius: 30,
},
glowInner: {
  position: "absolute",
  top: -1, left: -1, right: -1, bottom: -1,
  borderRadius: 17,
  borderWidth: 1,
  borderColor: "rgba(6,182,212,0.3)",
},

Actions

Action Icon Library
Share Share2 expo-sharing
Save Download Alert confirmation
Favorite Heart Zustand toggleFavorite

Gallery Screen

FlatList of generated memes with the MemeCard component.

MemeCard Component

Card (row layout)
├── Image (80x80 thumbnail)
├── Content
│   ├── Overlay text (1 line, truncated)
│   └── Date
└── Actions
    ├── Heart (toggle favorite)
    └── Trash2 (delete)

The gallery uses keyExtractor on item.id and renders with gap: 12 spacing between cards.

Empty State

When no memes exist, a centered message encourages the user to start generating.

Settings Screen

Three sections: API Key, Generation defaults, and About.

API Key Section

The API key input features a secure text field with eye toggle:

apiKeyRow
├── TextInput (secureTextEntry toggled)
└── Eye/EyeOff toggle button

apiKeyActions
├── "Save to Keychain" button (cyan)
└── "Remove" button (red, conditional)
NoteSecureField Pattern

The secureTextEntry prop is toggled via the eye icon, allowing users to verify their API key before saving. The key is stored in expo-secure-store which uses iOS Keychain / Android Keystore.

Generation Defaults

  • Resolution picker: 4 options (0.5K, 1K, 2K, 4K) — horizontally laid out
  • Aspect ratio picker: 3 options (1:1, 16:9, 9:16) — horizontally laid out
  • Auto overlay text: Switch toggle with cyan track color

About Section

Simple key-value rows showing app name, version, model (Nano Banana 2), and author (KaosMaps). Separated by subtle cyan-tinted dividers.

State Management
Source Code
---
title: "UI Screens"
---

## Screen Overview

Four tab screens compose the mobile experience:

```{mermaid}
graph LR
    G[Generate] -->|success| R[Result]
    G -->|no API key| S[Settings]
    R -->|back| G
    GL[Gallery] --> R
```

## Home Screen (Generate)

The primary screen with prompt input, overlay text, resolution picker, and generate button.

### Layout Structure

```
SafeAreaView
└── ScrollView (keyboardShouldPersistTaps)
    ├── Header (Sparkles icon + "NanoMeme")
    ├── Subtitle
    ├── Prompt Section
    │   ├── TextInput (multiline, 4 lines)
    │   └── "Load Yoda Preset" button
    ├── Overlay Text Section
    │   └── TextInput (single line)
    ├── Resolution Picker
    │   └── Row of 4 buttons (0.5K | 1K | 2K | 4K)
    ├── Error Card (conditional)
    └── Generate Button (with ActivityIndicator)
```

### Generation Flow

```typescript
const handleGenerate = useCallback(async () => {
  const apiKey = await getApiKey();
  if (!apiKey) {
    failGeneration("No API key configured...");
    router.navigate("/settings");
    return;
  }

  startGeneration();
  try {
    const result = await generateAndSaveMeme(
      apiKey,
      currentPrompt || DEFAULT_YODA_PROMPT,
      overlayText || DEFAULT_OVERLAY_TEXT,
    );
    completeGeneration(result.imageUri);
    router.navigate("/result");
  } catch (error) {
    failGeneration(
      error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Generation failed",
    );
  }
}, [currentPrompt, overlayText, ...]);
```

Key behaviors:

1. **No API key** → Redirects to Settings tab
2. **Empty prompt** → Falls back to Yoda preset prompt
3. **Success** → Navigates to Result screen
4. **Error** → Shows error card with red border

### KaosMaps Styling

All input fields use the dark theme with cyan-tinted borders:

```typescript
textArea: {
  backgroundColor: colors.surface.card,  // #1a1a24
  borderRadius: 12,
  padding: 14,
  color: colors.text.primary,            // #e2e8f0
  borderWidth: 1,
  borderColor: "rgba(6,182,212,0.15)",   // Cyan subtle border
},
```

## Result Screen

Displays the generated meme with share/save/favorite actions.

### KaosMaps Glow Effect

The image container features the signature KaosMaps glow:

```typescript
glowOuter: {
  position: "absolute",
  top: -2, left: -2, right: -2, bottom: -2,
  borderRadius: 18,
  shadowColor: colors.brand.cyan,
  shadowOffset: { width: 0, height: 0 },
  shadowOpacity: 0.15,
  shadowRadius: 30,
},
glowInner: {
  position: "absolute",
  top: -1, left: -1, right: -1, bottom: -1,
  borderRadius: 17,
  borderWidth: 1,
  borderColor: "rgba(6,182,212,0.3)",
},
```

### Actions

| Action | Icon | Library |
|--------|------|---------|
| Share | `Share2` | `expo-sharing` |
| Save | `Download` | Alert confirmation |
| Favorite | `Heart` | Zustand `toggleFavorite` |

## Gallery Screen

FlatList of generated memes with the `MemeCard` component.

### MemeCard Component

```
Card (row layout)
├── Image (80x80 thumbnail)
├── Content
│   ├── Overlay text (1 line, truncated)
│   └── Date
└── Actions
    ├── Heart (toggle favorite)
    └── Trash2 (delete)
```

The gallery uses `keyExtractor` on `item.id` and renders with `gap: 12` spacing between cards.

### Empty State

When no memes exist, a centered message encourages the user to start generating.

## Settings Screen

Three sections: API Key, Generation defaults, and About.

### API Key Section

The API key input features a secure text field with eye toggle:

```
apiKeyRow
├── TextInput (secureTextEntry toggled)
└── Eye/EyeOff toggle button

apiKeyActions
├── "Save to Keychain" button (cyan)
└── "Remove" button (red, conditional)
```

::: {.callout-note}
## SecureField Pattern
The `secureTextEntry` prop is toggled via the eye icon, allowing users to verify their API key before saving. The key is stored in `expo-secure-store` which uses iOS Keychain / Android Keystore.
:::

### Generation Defaults

- **Resolution picker**: 4 options (0.5K, 1K, 2K, 4K) — horizontally laid out
- **Aspect ratio picker**: 3 options (1:1, 16:9, 9:16) — horizontally laid out
- **Auto overlay text**: Switch toggle with cyan track color

### About Section

Simple key-value rows showing app name, version, model (Nano Banana 2), and author (KaosMaps). Separated by subtle cyan-tinted dividers.

NanoMeme Compendium

Built with Quarto by KaosMaps
